column1: AQUIFER ZONE CLASSIFICATION  —  Benin Formation, Delta State  (Zones I–III with distributed parameters)

column15: Very High – directly recharged, near surface
column16: Poor – insufficient saturated thickness, contamination risk
column18: Thickness and lateral extent of surface clay cap

column15: Moderate – some clay baffles; pollution reaches within months–years
column16: Excellent – primary production zone; most boreholes screened here
column18: Lateral continuity of sand bodies; clay baffle thickness and extent

column16: Very Good – high yield; used for urban/industrial supply
column18: Lignite marker continuity; iron contamination risk (Fe>0.3 mg/L zone)

column15: Very Low – deep and well confined
column16: Good but costly to drill; risk of Fe-rich water
column18: Connectivity with Zone II; Fe mineralisation sources

column1: ?  Zone definitions calibrated to Delta State lithologic dataset (this study) and published hydraulic parameter ranges (Amajor 1991; Aweto & Akpoborie 2015; Anomohanran 2014,2021; Ofomola et al. 2022,2024).
